text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Teams break their work into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. Each sprint focuses on completing a set of tasks from the backlog, which is a prioritized list of features and fixes. Every day, the team holds a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. To define what needs to be done, the team writes user stories – simple descriptions of a feature from the user's perspective. This is known as iterative development, because the team improves the product step-by-step through each sprint, based on feedback and changing requirements.
